rationale: go build ./... is clean. go test ./... in one parallel full run showed 3 timeout FAILs (harness, instruct, validate), each hitting the default 10m alarm. internal/instruct's is TestRun_IdleNoBeads, the known z4ps flake per the BRIEF. internal/harness's TestLLM_CompleteFromDoomedWorktree and internal/validate's TestValidateSpec_ADRTouchpointExtractionBoundary are NOT named in the BRIEF's accepted-flake list, so I isolated them: run alone with -timeout 5m, both PASS cleanly (harness 217s incl. a real subprocess agent invocation; validate 93s across all subtests). Neither package is touched by the spec-113 diff (confirmed via file list below), so these are full-suite parallel resource-contention timeouts (subprocess-heavy LLM-harness/bd-CLI tests starved of CPU/IO when ~40 packages run concurrently), not regressions from R1-R4. `git diff --stat 45ae7577 12c02de1` = 109 files changed, exactly matching the BRIEF's inventory: cmd/mindspec/panel.go+panel_test.go, internal/panel/create.go+create_test.go+panel_test.go, internal/config/config.go+config_test.go, plugins/mindspec/workflows/ms-panel.js + .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js mirror + plugins/mindspec/workflow_test.go, .mindspec/domains/workflow/interfaces.md, spec.md/plan.md, .beads/issues.jsonl (bead-status metadata), and the rest tracked review-artifact JSONs. No unexpected code file changed.

findings.description: A single parallel `go test ./...` run produces 3 timeout FAILs, not 1: internal/harness (TestLLM_CompleteFromDoomedWorktree, hung on a subprocess Wait at 4m14s before the 10m alarm) and internal/validate (TestValidateSpec_ADRTouchpointExtractionBoundary, hung inside bd-CLI ListJSON) in addition to the BRIEF-acknowledged internal/instruct/TestRun_IdleNoBeads. Isolated reruns of the harness and validate tests (`go test -run <name> -timeout 5m`) both PASS in well under the timeout (217s and 93s respectively), and neither package appears in the spec-113 diff, so this reads as parallel-suite resource contention (CPU/subprocess starvation from concurrently running many subprocess-heavy packages) rather than a code regression. Not blocking, but worth a follow-up bead to make `go test ./...` reliably green in one shot (e.g. lower default -p for CI, or bump -timeout for subprocess-heavy suites) so future final-review S1 lenses don't need to hand-verify isolation every time.
